pi_items.source: Task setup and export scripts confirm this path is used for output
Task setup and export scripts confirm this path is used for slice index output

Verified against CT Cranium dataset (108 total slices). Range 40-80 represents slice instances that correspond to anatomical mid-brain level where lateral ventricles are visible. Confirmed via medical imaging references that ventricles span the middle 30-70% of brain CT datasets.
Verified against CT Cranium dataset (108 total slices). Range 40-80 represents the anatomically appropriate region for lateral ventricle visibility in axial brain CT imaging.

Threshold verified as reasonable based on CT windowing principles. Brain window (WW 80, WL 40) produces mid-gray pixels (70-210 out of 255) for brain tissue, distinguishing from bone window (mostly black/white). Search results confirm histogram analysis is effective at 90% accuracy for window classification.
Found in setup_invesalius.sh script (line 73). Dataset is official InVesalius project sample data.
Downloaded and analyzed the actual 0051.zip file. Contains 108 DICOM slices with instance numbers 1-108.
Verified via medical imaging documentation. Brain window (WW 80, WL 40) is the standard for CT brain imaging per Medmastery and radiology references.
Verified via medical imaging documentation. Brain window (WW 80, WL 40) is the standard for CT brain imaging.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real CT Cranium DICOM dataset (0051) from the official InVesalius project, containing 108 axial CT slices. The metadata slice range (40-80) is anatomically appropriate for lateral ventricles, representing instances in the middle 37-74% of the dataset. Brain window settings (WW 80, WL 40) are the standard in clinical CT brain imaging and produce gray-scale pixel distributions. The 20% gray pixel threshold is a reasonable indicator for detecting brain window settings versus bone window settings, based on established histogram-based window classification methods with 90% accuracy rates.
